scenario: Helios Energy bills 250000 customers per month in Camunda 8.8. While a meter reading user task is open, the team wants the process to send a reminder every 24 hours up to 3 times without cancelling the user task, after which the reminders must stop while the user task itself remains active for completion.
question: Which timer definition on a non-interrupting boundary event sends a reminder every 24 hours for a maximum of three iterations?

options.text: A time cycle definition of R3/PT24H on a non-interrupting boundary timer that reschedules itself after each firing until three repetitions have been reached and then stops scheduling
A time duration definition of PT24H on a non-interrupting boundary timer that reschedules itself every 24 hours indefinitely until the attached user task is completed by the assignee
A time cycle definition of R3/PT24H on an interrupting boundary timer that triggers the reminder branch and cancels the meter reading user task after the third iteration fires
A time date definition that points at a single instant 24 hours after activation on a non-interrupting boundary timer that fires three reminder events from that single date value

a.text: Correct. A non-interrupting boundary timer defined with a time cycle and repetitions greater than zero reschedules itself after each firing until the defined number of repetitions is reached, so R3/PT24H gives three reminders without cancelling the attached user task.

b.text: Incorrect. A non-interrupting timer boundary event defined with a time duration triggers only a single time once the date is reached; it does not reschedule itself for further reminders.

c.text: Incorrect. Interrupting boundary timers terminate the attached activity when they fire, so the meter reading user task would be cancelled rather than left active for the assignee.

d.text: Incorrect. A time date definition points at a single instant rather than a repeating schedule, so it cannot produce three separate reminder events on the boundary event.

explanation: Non-interrupting timer boundary events accept a duration, a cycle, or a date. Only the time cycle with repetitions greater than zero reschedules itself until the configured number of repetitions has fired, so R3/PT24H is the documented pattern for three reminders while the attached activity stays active.
